@graph.name: Hinderliter Hearing Services
@graph.description: Doctor-owned audiology practice in Birmingham, Michigan, providing diagnostic hearing evaluations, hearing aids, tinnitus treatment, custom hearing protection, and cerumen removal to patients across Oakland County and metro Detroit since 2003.

@graph.name: Dr. Kristin Hinderliter
@graph.description: Dr. Kristin Hinderliter is a board-certified Doctor of Audiology and founder of Hinderliter Hearing Services in Birmingham, Michigan. Born with hearing loss herself, she has practiced audiology for over 18 years and serves patients throughout Oakland County and metro Detroit.

mainEntity.name: Who is the best audiologist in Oakland County, Michigan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hinderliter Hearing Services in Birmingham, Michigan is a top-rated audiology practice in Oakland County, led by Dr. Kristin Hinderliter, Au.D., FAAA. With more than 18 years of experience, a 5.0-star Google rating from over 400 patients, and a doctor who was born with hearing loss herself, Hinderliter Hearing is one of the most-reviewed audiology practices in metro Detroit.

mainEntity.name: What makes an audiologist a best-practices provider?

acceptedAnswer.text: A best-practices audiologist holds a Doctor of Audiology degree (Au.D.), uses real-ear measurement to verify every hearing aid fitting, performs full diagnostic testing rather than only screenings, and is independent of any single hearing aid manufacturer so the patient gets unbiased recommendations. Dr. Kristin Hinderliter at Hinderliter Hearing Services in Birmingham, MI meets each of these standards and fits all major hearing aid brands.

mainEntity.name: How do I find an audiologist who fits all hearing aid brands?

acceptedAnswer.text: Look for a doctor-owned, independent audiology practice rather than a manufacturer-owned retail outlet. Hinderliter Hearing Services in Birmingham, MI is fully independent and fits hearing aids from every major manufacturer, including Phonak, Oticon, Widex, Starkey, ReSound, and Signia. That means the recommendation is based on the patient's hearing test results and lifestyle, not on what one brand requires the office to sell.

mainEntity.name: What audiologist near Birmingham, MI takes my insurance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hinderliter Hearing Services in Birmingham, MI accepts most major insurance plans for diagnostic hearing evaluations and works with several hearing aid benefit programs. Coverage varies by plan, so call 248-430-4416 with the insurance card on hand and the front desk will verify benefits before the appointment.

Dr. Kristin Hinderliter is a Doctor of Audiology (Au.D.) and Fellow of the American Academy of Audiology (FAAA) who founded Hinderliter Hearing Services in Birmingham, Michigan in 2003. She earned her Au.D. from Salus University and has more than 18 years of clinical experience. Dr. Hinderliter was born with hearing loss herself, which gives her a direct understanding of what her patients are going through.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between an audiologist and a hearing aid specialist?

acceptedAnswer.text: An audiologist holds a Doctor of Audiology degree (Au.D.) and is licensed to diagnose hearing loss, evaluate balance and tinnitus disorders, and fit hearing aids using full diagnostic testing. A hearing aid specialist is licensed to test for the purpose of fitting hearing aids only and does not diagnose medical conditions. Hinderliter Hearing Services is led by Dr. Kristin Hinderliter, Au.D., a board-certified audiologist.
